route.name: Ojai Rim Loop
route.description: Public GPX Southern California overnighter with big climbs, dirt roads/trails, pavement connectors, and exposed chaparral terrain.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Southern California overnighter with big climbs, dirt roads/trails, pavement connectors, and exposed chaparral terrain.
route.bestSeason: Cool season or mild shoulder-season weather; July is normally too hot.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Southern California overnighter with big climbs, dirt roads/trails, pavement connectors, and exposed chaparral terrain.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water is uncertain in summer and must be verified before any attempt.
resources.food: Ojai services before/after; carry route food.
resources.camping: Los Padres/route camp options require current access, fire, and water confirmation.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Keep as a cool-season California route.
Reject for next weekend unless forecast is unusually mild.

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Ojai backcountry can be brutally hot in July; reject for next weekend without a cool coastal forecast and confirmed water.
